titleStmt.title: Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.45

div.p: David by the grace of God king of the Scots to all his barons, and men, and friends, French and English, greeting. Know me to have given and conceded Annandale [Estrahanent] to Robert de Brus, and the whole land from the boundary of Dunegal of Nithsdale [Stranit] as far as the boundary of Ranulf le Meschin. And I will and concede that he hold and have the land and its castle well and honourably with all its customs, namely, with all those customs which Ranulf le Meschin ever had in Carlisle and his land of Cumberland on that day in which he ever had them best and most freely. As witness: Eustace fitz John; Hugh de Morville; Alan de Percy; William de Somerville; Berengar Engaine; Ranulf de Soules; Walter de Morville; Hervey fitz Warin; Edmund the chamberlain. At Scone.
